summary: Iter-9 testing PASS. Verified the getUserMedia-race fix (mediaReady + readyPromise + pendingOffer). Found and fixed a one-line miss in CallScreen.jsx: the ref `const mediaReady = useRef(null)` was used (3 sites) but never declared, which would have thrown ReferenceError on mount of the user CallScreen and broken the whole call flow. After adding the declaration, baseline v8 suite is back to 39/39 (one transient flake on `user_sees_reject_toast` on first run was a 1.5s timing race — passed cleanly on the immediate re-run, identical to iter-8 behaviour). New /app/tests/playwright_call_test_v9_race.py installs a 600-800ms delay on navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia via addInitScript on BOTH user and provider contexts, then races accept within ~10-60ms of the incoming popup. 3 back-to-back iterations all PASS (15/15): WS frame inspection shows webrtc_offer is followed by webrtc_answer within 0.6-0.7s even when getUserMedia is artificially slow, and the LARGEST <video> on BOTH the user and provider pages reaches videoTracks=1 + readyState=4 within the 6s ICE window. This confirms ProviderCallScreen's pendingOffer queueing works: offer arrives before provider's media is ready, gets buffered in pendingOffer.current, then handleOffer fires once readyPromise resolves.
action_items: ACK the one-line fix testing agent applied: `const mediaReady = useRef(null)` added to /app/frontend/src/pages/CallScreen.jsx (line 39). Without this, the file would have thrown ReferenceError on mount because mediaReady.current was referenced inside init() and inside offAccept. Main agent should fold this into their next checkpoint so the fix is intentional, not relying on the test agent's patch.
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL (fixed by testing agent): CallScreen.jsx referenced `mediaReady.current` on lines 55, 65, 88 but never declared the ref. The provider-side equivalent `readyPromise` + `pendingOffer` ARE properly declared. This is an asymmetric implementation miss — most likely an incomplete patch when the fix was applied to both files. Added `const mediaReady = useRef(null)` below `hideTimer`.

The pendingOffer queueing on ProviderCallScreen is correct: signaling.on('webrtc_offer') is registered synchronously (line 70) in the same useEffect that kicks off readyPromise (line 52), so any early offer is captured. The 'if (!localStream.current || !pc.current) pendingOffer.current = sdp' gate (line 71) cleanly handles the race. Confirmed by WS frame inspection in the race test.

Minor consistency nit: ProviderCallScreen does `if (!localStream.current || !pc.current)` for the offer-queue guard, but a user with the camera blocked (no localStream) would never have pc.current populated (preparePeer is awaited inside readyPromise). The guard is technically redundant when pc.current is null since preparePeer always runs even on getUserMedia failure. Not a bug, just minor.

Pre-existing (carried from iter-8): /provider/earnings still shows ₹0 because provider-side endCall persists to api.saveCallLog but ProviderEarnings page reads from a different aggregator. Out of scope for this iter.

Race test approach: slow-GUM via addInitScript before page.goto is the right way to deterministically reproduce the bug in headless — the real-mobile race comes from the OS-level camera HAL being slow, not from JS-level delays, but the effect on SDP negotiation is identical.
seed_data_creation: None — race test does localStorage.clear() + fresh login per iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/tests/playwright_call_test_v9_race.py to stress-test future webrtc/signaling changes. The slow-GUM init script reliably triggers the offer-before-ready race (offer lands within ~10ms of accept, provider GUM takes 600-800ms). If a future change breaks pendingOffer queueing, this test will catch it — the provider remote video will be missing videoTracks. If only ONE side is missing tracks, distinguish: (a) user-side missing => mediaReady gate broken on caller; (b) provider-side missing => pendingOffer queue/preparePeer ordering broken. v8 suite remains the regression baseline (39/39). Note: `user_sees_reject_toast` is mildly flaky (~1 in 6 runs) due to a 1.5s sleep after reject — if it fails, re-run once before reporting.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE of the original 'sometimes user side not visible on provider view' bug: classic getUserMedia <-> WebRTC SDP negotiation race. Sequence on slow mobile: (1) user clicks call -> offAccept fires synchronously on call_accept WS frame -> startPeer(true) builds RTCPeerConnection -> addTrack iterates localStream.current.getTracks() which is STILL NULL because getUserMedia is pending -> createOffer produces SDP with no m=video sections -> answer mirrors empty media -> both pages connect but exchange no media. FIX: the user-side awaits mediaReady (a promise that resolves only after getUserMedia returns) before calling startPeer; the provider-side awaits readyPromise (gum+preparePeer) before handleOffer, with pendingOffer queueing any webrtc_offer that arrives before readiness. Verified empirically with 600-800ms artificial GUM delay across 3 stress iterations — both sides reach videoTracks=1 within 6s of accept, offer->answer roundtrip ~0.6s. INCOMPLETE PATCH bug discovered: the user-side fix was missing the ref declaration `const mediaReady = useRef(null)`, which I added.
